title: ischaemum indicum subvar. malacophyllum
intro: Ischaemum indicum subvar. malacophyllum, also known as White Grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to tropical Asia. The plant is known to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of White Grass include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds and coughs, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of White Grass can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
